Primary Decisions Owned: Confirms availability; provides site/readiness info; approves placement/sign-off; reports issues.
Must Be Informed When: Appointment or readiness changes; no-go decisions; install limitations; post-install ownership/timeline changes.
Failure Impact If Missed: Missed expectations; poor prep; disputes; weak sign-off; complaint escalation.
Inputs Needed: Appointment details; prep instructions; remedy timeline.
Outputs Produced: Availability; photos/site info; access; sign-off; complaint details.
Notes / Jargon: Customer actions are operational inputs, not just CX context.

Primary Decisions Owned: Confirms deliverability and sets appointment when the order is clean enough to schedule.
Must Be Informed When: Order data conflicts with install reality or promised service needs correction.
Failure Impact If Missed: Bad appointment set; downstream install failure; route waste.
Inputs Needed: Order attributes; contact data; service-level details.
Outputs Produced: Confirmed appointment; corrected order attributes; schedule commitments.
Escalation Authority: Limited; escalates to operations management on serviceability conflicts.
Notes / Jargon: Stage-1 risk screening is more than admin work.

Primary Decisions Owned: Verifies readiness; flags site/spec/access issues before dispatch.
Must Be Informed When: Readiness gaps change route feasibility; safety or code concerns appear.
Failure Impact If Missed: Crew arrives to a non-installable site; route loss; avoidable reschedule.
Inputs Needed: Photos; specs; customer notes; utility requirements.
Outputs Produced: Readiness status; exception notes; route-feasibility flags.
Notes / Jargon: This is where many failures are prevented before the truck leaves.

Primary Decisions Owned: Owns route assignment; crew capacity decisions; resequencing; same-day route tradeoffs.
Must Be Informed When: Readiness changes route feasibility; shortages appear; field issues affect downstream stops; revisit/handoff is needed.
Failure Impact If Missed: Late route; missed windows; wrong crew/tools; cascading stop failure.
Inputs Needed: Confirmed install notes; crew capacity; geography; inventory status; field updates.
Outputs Produced: Route assignment; manifest notes; resequencing decisions; field support.
Escalation Authority: Escalates shortages or conflicts beyond dispatch authority.
Notes / Jargon: Route feasibility is an operational judgment, not just a software output.

Primary Decisions Owned: Verifies product; parts; and accessories are on the right route.
Must Be Informed When: Shortages; misloads; or missing kits are found during load verification.
Failure Impact If Missed: Failed stop; wrong route; same-day rework.
Inputs Needed: Route manifest; SKU/accessory requirements; install notes.
Outputs Produced: Verified load record; shortage flags; corrected manifest.
Escalation Authority: Typically no final escalation authority; hands issues to dispatch/ops.
Notes / Jargon: Critical for preventing hidden same-day failures.

Primary Decisions Owned: Makes field go/no-go; removal; install; testing; and sign-off decisions within authority.
Must Be Informed When: Site mismatch; safety issue; scope dispute; install limitation; test failure; customer dispute.
Failure Impact If Missed: Unsafe work; damage; incomplete install; weak evidence trail; complaint escalation.
Inputs Needed: Order packet; site notes; route notes; tools; install kit; customer presence.
Outputs Produced: Field notes; photos; stop disposition; sign-off; issue classification signal.
Escalation Authority: Escalates when safety; code; damage; scope; or authority boundaries are crossed.
Notes / Jargon: Crew judgment is the key operational control point in stages 4-7.

Primary Decisions Owned: Resolves serviceability conflicts; major route conflicts; safety/cost exceptions; high-severity recovery decisions.
Must Be Informed When: Order conflicts; safety/code issues; route-breaking exceptions; major complaints or recovery cost cases.
Failure Impact If Missed: Wrong issue owner; unsafe completion; avoidable cost; poor recovery experience.
Inputs Needed: Escalation notes; photos; cost/risk context; route impact.
Notes / Jargon: Authority boundary owner for non-standard decisions.

Primary Decisions Owned: Advises on code/product/install issues that exceed normal field authority.
Must Be Informed When: Code questions; product defects; non-standard hookup decisions.
Failure Impact If Missed: Unsafe install or wrong workaround; repeat visits.
Inputs Needed: Photos; defect details; install conditions; code issue notes.
Escalation Authority: Advisory or delegated depending on org design.
Notes / Jargon: Most visible when install stops being standard.

Primary Decisions Owned: Owns communication-heavy post-install cases and customer-facing recovery updates.
Must Be Informed When: Issue is reported after the visit; ownership/timeline must be communicated.
Failure Impact If Missed: Dropped cases; customer confusion; repeated contacts.
Inputs Needed: Complaint details; install notes; promised timeline; service history.
Outputs Produced: Case ownership; customer updates; routed follow-up.
Escalation Authority: Limited; escalates high-severity or cost issues.
Notes / Jargon: Fast ownership often matters more to the customer than instant resolution.

Primary Decisions Owned: Owns revisit/rework when the issue requires field remedy.
Must Be Informed When: Post-install issue requires physical remedy or technical revisit.
Failure Impact If Missed: Long open cases; repeated failures; duplicated visits.
Inputs Needed: Case file; original install evidence; parts/tools requirements.
Outputs Produced: Revisit completion; rework notes; remedy proof.
Escalation Authority: Escalates high-severity or ambiguous cases to management.
Notes / Jargon: Triggered when post-install resolution becomes a new field workflow.
